name: Research Desk
description: 3 web-research capabilities + 2 connector tool-reactions (Airtable, Notion). Exercises fan-out #1 (5 independent capability resolutions) and fan-out #2 (2 real connector tool-tests). The web-research caps are native (no credential); the two reactions bind to healthy Airtable + Notion vault credentials.
intent:
Build me a Web Research Desk agent. It has FIVE capabilities:

(1) Topic sweep — given a research topic or question, use web search and web fetch to gather the most relevant recent sources, extract the key facts from each page, and return a ranked, de-duplicated source list with a one-paragraph synthesis. Always cite each source URL.

(2) Scheduled feed scan — on a daily schedule, re-run the tracked topics, pull new items from RSS/Atom feeds and fresh web searches, drop anything already seen, and summarise only what is new.

(3) Deep-dive synthesis — given a set of URLs, fetch and extract each one, then produce a structured briefing (background, key findings, open questions, sources).

(4) Log findings to Airtable — whenever a vetted finding is produced, append it as a row to an Airtable base that tracks research (fields: title, url, topic, summary, captured_at). Use the Airtable connector.

(5) Publish digest to Notion — when a digest or briefing is ready, write it as a page/database entry in Notion so the team can read it. Use the Notion connector.

Web search and web fetch are native tools — do NOT install SerpAPI/Tavily or any external search connector. No human review needed for reads; the Airtable and Notion writes are the only side-effecting steps.

hard_assertions: credentialLinks resolves 'airtable' to a vault credential id
credentialLinks resolves 'notion' to a vault credential id
no capability requires a credential for web search/fetch (native only)
no hallucinated external search connector (serpapi/tavily/google_search) present

rubric.note: 0-3 per dimension, judged by a Claude-Code-as-judge pass (athena style). Not a substitute for the hard_assertions above — this scores QUALITY, the assertions gate CORRECTNESS.

dimensions.prompt: Do the resolved capabilities cover all five requested jobs (sweep, scheduled scan, deep-dive, Airtable log, Notion publish) without collapsing distinct jobs into one or padding with invented ones?
Are the 5 capabilities genuinely distinct (different triggers/inputs/outputs), or near-duplicates?

Do the Airtable and Notion capabilities reference the CORRECT connector (service_type airtable / notion), name the right tool in tool_hints, and carry a resolved credentialLink? Penalise if a write is described in prose but no connector tool is bound.

Are triggers sensible per capability — scheduled scan is a schedule; the two writes are event/reaction or chained off a finding; sweep/deep-dive are manual? Penalise a scheduled trigger on the interactive sweep, etc.
Are all tools real (native web tools + http_request + the two connectors)? Penalise any hallucinated tool, invented connector, or a web-search step that wrongly requires a credential.

notes: Requires healthy Airtable + Notion credentials in the vault (service_type 'airtable' and 'notion', one non-empty non-last-failed row each) for the connector tool-tests to go green and credentialLinks to resolve. Without them the build still promotes but the two reactions report credential_missing and expect_tool_test flips to 'credential_missing' — the harness records that state rather than failing, so structure/quality are still comparable across variants.
